headline: Why Is Robinhood (HOOD) Stock Soaring Today
summary: Shares of financial services company Robinhood (NASDAQ:HOOD) jumped 6% in the afternoon session after it benefited from a confluence of positive catalysts, including strong interest in its prediction markets tied to the 2026 FIFA World Cup, multiple bullish analyst actions, and a supportive macro environment.

content.title: Why Is Robinhood (HOOD) Stock Soaring Today
content.summary: Shares of financial services company Robinhood (NASDAQ:HOOD) jumped 6% in the afternoon session after it benefited from a confluence of positive catalysts, including strong interest in its prediction markets tied to the 2026 FIFA World Cup, multiple bullish analyst actions, and a supportive macro environment.

headline: HOOD Stock Pulls Back Overnight After AI Trading Launch – Insider Sales Draw Attention
summary: A filing with the SEC revealed that CFO Shiv Verma had sold shares worth $393,778 of the company’s Class A common stock on Monday.

headline: Robinhood’s note on 10% layoffs shows blaming AI isn’t cutting it
summary: Unlike many of his tech industry peers who have cut thousands of jobs citing the need to restructure to make the most of AI, Robinhood's CEO Vlad Tenev conspicuously made no mention of AI in his note about layoffs.

content.title: Robinhood’s note on 10% layoffs shows blaming AI isn’t cutting it
content.summary: Unlike many of his tech industry peers who have cut thousands of jobs citing the need to restructure to make the most of AI, Robinhood's CEO Vlad Tenev conspicuously made no mention of AI in his note about layoffs.

headline: Yum to Sell Pizza Hut; Dave & Buster's Sinks | Stock Movers
summary: On this episode of Stock Movers with Alexis Christoforous: - Yum! Brands (YUM) agreed to sell its struggling Pizza Hut chain for $2.7 billion to a private equity firm and its China business. LongRange Capital will acquire Pizza Hut ex-China for $1.5 billion, Yum said in a statement Tuesday. Yum China Holdings Inc. will buy the rest of the business for $1.2 billion. The transactions are expected to close in the third quarter. - Dave & Buster's (PLAY) shares tumble after the operator of restaurant and entertainment facilities reported comparable sales for the first quarter that missed the average analyst estimate. Research firm Benchmark downgraded the stock, citing a lack of confidence that same-store sales will stabilize/eventually turn positive this year. - Robinhood Markets (HOOD) shares are up after it said it is reducing its full-time employee workforce by 10%, and also closing a small number of open roles.

content.title: Yum to Sell Pizza Hut; Dave & Buster's Sinks | Stock Movers
content.description:
On this episode of Stock Movers with Alexis Christoforous:

- Yum! Brands (YUM) agreed to sell its struggling Pizza Hut chain for $2.7 billion to a private equity firm and its China business. LongRange Capital will acquire Pizza Hut ex-China for $1.5 billion, Yum said in a statement Tuesday. Yum China Holdings Inc. will buy the rest of the business for $1.2 billion. The transactions are expected to close in the third quarter.

- Dave & Buster's (PLAY) shares tumble after the operator of restaurant and entertainment facilities reported comparable sales for the first quarter that missed the average analyst estimate. Research firm Benchmark downgraded the stock, citing a lack of confidence that same-store sales will stabilize/eventually turn positive this year. 

- Robinhood Markets (HOOD) shares are up after it said it is reducing its full-time employee workforce by 10%, and also closing a small number of open roles.
content.summary: On this episode of Stock Movers with Alexis Christoforous: - Yum! Brands (YUM) agreed to sell its struggling Pizza Hut chain for $2.7 billion to a private equity firm and its China business. LongRange Capital will acquire Pizza Hut ex-China for $1.5 billion, Yum said in a statement Tuesday. Yum China Holdings Inc. will buy the rest of the business for $1.2 billion. The transactions are expected to close in the third quarter. - Dave & Buster's (PLAY) shares tumble after the operator of restaurant and entertainment facilities reported comparable sales for the first quarter that missed the average analyst estimate. Research firm Benchmark downgraded the stock, citing a lack of confidence that same-store sales will stabilize/eventually turn positive this year. - Robinhood Markets (HOOD) shares are up after it said it is reducing its full-time employee workforce by 10%, and also closing a small number of open roles.

headline: Robinhood Is Cutting 10% of Its Workforce but It’s Not Why You Think
summary: Robinhood Markets  stock was gaining early Tuesday after the trading platform said it would cut around a tenth of its employees.  It’s just the latest technology company to announce significant cuts to its workforce.  Robinhood said it would cut about 10% of its full-time employees and close a small number of open roles.

content.title: Robinhood Is Cutting 10% of Its Workforce but It’s Not Why You Think
content.summary: Robinhood Markets  stock was gaining early Tuesday after the trading platform said it would cut around a tenth of its employees.  It’s just the latest technology company to announce significant cuts to its workforce.  Robinhood said it would cut about 10% of its full-time employees and close a small number of open roles.

headline: Robinhood cuts 10% of staff as CEO Tenev pushes for ’lean’ team
summary: Investing.com -- Robinhood Markets (NASDAQ:HOOD) shares rose 2% on Tuesday after the trading platform announced it would cut 10% of its full-time workforce, or approximately 290 roles, as part of efforts to streamline operations and flatten management layers.
